security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Malicious votes could alter the proposal outcome.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Unsigned votes can be easily manipulated.

assumptions: The governance contract does not validate vote integrity.
The actor can submit arbitrarily crafted vote messages.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust vote validation and tamper-evident mechanisms.

description: An attacker may alter the contents of a vote to manipulate the outcome of a proposal.

security_gap.gap_description: The system lacks mechanisms to ensure vote accountability.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Lack of proper logging and auditing makes tracing votes difficult.

assumptions: The system does not log or record individual votes with sufficient user traceability.
The system does not have mechanisms for non-repudiation.

mitigation.strategy: Implement secure logging, auditing, and chain-based voting records with immutable timestamps and user identifiers.

description: An attacker may cast a vote and later deny having done so.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes might be exposed to unauthorized parties.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Insufficient access control mechanisms may expose vote data.

assumptions: The governance contract does not protect vote confidentiality.
The actor can gain access to the voting data through vulnerabilities in the system.

mitigation.strategy: Encrypt vote data both in transit and at rest and implement robust access control measures.

description: An attacker might gain access to sensitive data related to votes during submission or storage.
